MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Anne. C. Gritzer, (415)781-7072 of Waslworth WFBM, LLP for Defendant/Moving party SFHA.
Katherine Kimsey, (415)667-3168 of Littler Mendelson, PC for Plaintiff/opposing party Charlene Finley.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims are barred by the statute of limitations. Order signed in open Court.

Katherine Kinesey, Esq., (415-677-31680 of Littler Mendelson for Plaintiff/Opposing part Charlene Finley.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Continued To Sep-30-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anne Gritzer, Esq. (415-781-7072) of WFBM for Defendant/ Moving party San Francisco Housing Authority.
No other appearance.

Defendant's Counsel submits to the tentative ruling. She reports Plaintiff has retained counsel and that counsel will not be appearing. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's Demurer to First Amended Complaint is continued to September 30, 2015 to give Plaintiff an opportunity to file and a Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Complaint. Such motion, if filed, should be notices in accordance with CCP 1005 and 1013. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2015 Continued To Aug-05-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charlene Finley (415-571-6238) for Plaintiff/opposing party in Pro Per.
Anne Gritzer, Esq. of WFBM (415-781-7072) for Defendant/ Moving party San FRancisco Housing Authority.

The Court informs Plaintiff the complaint has no facts and Plaintiff is not entitled to punitive damages. The Court suggests Plaintiff to get someone since she may lose on Procedure. The Defendant's Claim on Punitive Damages is sustained without leave to amend. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts regarding each element of cause of action for negligence. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file and serve an amended complaint. Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

Defendant San Francisco Housing Authoritys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jun-12-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charlene Finley (415-571-6238) for Plaintiff/opposing party in Pro Per.
Anne Gritzer, Esq. of WFBM (415-781-7072) for Defendant/ Moving party San FRancisco Housing Authority.

The Court informs Plaintiff the complaint has no facts and Plaintiff is not entitled to punitive damages. The Court suggests Plaintiff to get someone since she may lose on Procedure. The Defendant's Claim on Punitive Damages is sustained without leave to amend. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages is Denied as moot in that the Court has sustained the demurrer to the complaint with leave to amend. Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CHARLENE FINLEY, TEL. NO. 571-6238, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is granted. Plaintiff has not properly effectuated service on Defendant. The request for dismissal is denied. (See CCP sec. 583.220(b).) All future motions in this case shall be noticed in Dept. 501, the real property/housing court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489
